00-001-e46f30ea.sentence: Population: patients with advanced Parkinson's disease and severe motor symptoms [#ev:dbs_vs_medical_therapy_rct:0-800].

00-004-573ec419.sentence: Primary endpoint: the changes from baseline to six months in the quality of life, as assessed by the Parkinson's Disease Questionnaire (PDQ-39), and the severity of symptoms without medication, according to the Unified Parkinson's Disease Rating Scale, part III (UPDRS-III) [#ev:dbs_vs_medical_therapy_rct:0-800].

01-001-da63a504.sentence: The Movement Disorder Society-sponsored Unified Parkinson's Disease Rating Scale, known as the MDS-UPDRS, structures the clinical assessment of Parkinson's disease into four distinct and complementary parts [#ev:parkinson_staging_progression:0-800].

01-002-08669ce3.sentence: Whereas Part I addresses the non-motor domain, Part II shifts the focus to motor aspects of daily function [#ev:parkinson_staging_progression:300-1100].

01-003-3c0be25d.sentence: However, Part IV specifically records motor complications, distinguishing them from the core motor examination [#ev:parkinson_staging_progression:300-1100].

01-004-aa0dfd07.sentence: The combined clinimetric results of this study support the validity of the MDS-UPDRS for rating PD [#ev:parkinson_staging_progression:600-1400].

01-005-25a7783e.sentence: The primary source identifies hardware complications as a distinct category of DBS-related adverse events [#ev:dbs_complications_warning_signs:0-800].

01-006-28d4a647.sentence: In contrast, the source does not offer guidance on daily life adjustments or support strategies for patients experiencing hardware complications [#ev:dbs_complications_warning_signs:0-800].

03-000-631e9a66.sentence: The Movement Disorder Society-sponsored revision of the Unified Parkinson's Disease Rating Scale (MDS-UPDRS), validated in a study of 877 patients from 39 sites, demonstrated high internal consistency (Cronbach's alpha = 0.79–0.93 across parts) and strong correlation with the original UPDRS (ρ = 0.96), providing a comprehensive framework for tracking these changes across four parts: non-motor experiences of daily living, motor experiences of daily living, motor examination, and motor complications [#ev:parkinson_staging_progression:300-1100].

03-001-9d60b6ff.sentence: In advanced stages, symptoms are severe and daily life usually requires full-time support [#ev:ev_014:0-800].

04-000-46c72d4a.sentence: The FDA labeling for carbidopa-levodopa explicitly warns that patients should be observed carefully when the dosage is reduced abruptly or discontinued, especially if they are receiving neuroleptics [#ev:dopaminergic_withdrawal_caution:11900-12700].

04-001-b299a8da.sentence: DBS hardware complications are relatively common; in a prospective series of 81 consecutive patients with PD undergoing subthalamic nucleus (STN) DBS followed for a mean of 17 months, hardware complications occurred in 26.2% of patients (atom_009) [#ev:dbs_complications_warning_signs:0-800].

04-002-7ecb0b18.sentence: Specific complications included infections requiring system removal in 2.5% (atom_010) and implantable pulse generator (IPG) removal in 3.7% (atom_011), misplaced leads in 12.5% (atom_012), and lead migration, fracture, or IPG malfunction [#ev:dbs_complications_warning_signs:100-900].

04-003-d104d610.sentence: A review of the FDA MAUDE database over 10 years identified 221 unique adverse events, with infection being the most common (16.2%) (atom_013) and lead migration accounting for 8.6% (atom_014) [#ev:ev_407:500-1300].

04-004-4c535361.sentence: Over a third (40%) of all devices with adverse events required return to the operating room for explant or revision (atom_003, atom_004) [#ev:ev_407:1300-2100].

04-005-fb4a5d8a.sentence: A review of postoperative psychosis after DBS reported a lifetime incidence of psychosis of 28.3% (atom_018) in PD patients following DBS, underscoring the need for vigilance [#ev:ev_731:3200-4000].

04-006-368ed658.sentence: Additionally, implantable pulse generator expiration can precipitate a movement disorder emergency with severe parkinsonian worsening or akinetic crisis, necessitating urgent medical evaluation [#ev:ev_246:700-1500].

04-007-e9e44993.sentence: Abrupt reduction or discontinuation of carbidopa-levodopa has been associated with a symptom complex resembling neuroleptic malignant syndrome (NMS), characterized by hyperthermia, muscle rigidity, altered consciousness, and autonomic dysfunction [#ev:dopaminergic_withdrawal_caution:12200-13000].

04-008-4c9449e3.sentence: Psychosis can emerge after STN DBS, as illustrated by clinical cases where patients developed paranoid ideation, hallucinations, and delusions within months of surgery, requiring antipsychotic treatment [#ev:ev_731:1900-2700].

04-009-c4c7ee16.sentence: Patients taking dopaminergic medications may experience intense urges to gamble, increased sexual urges, binge eating, and other compulsive behaviors, which may resolve with dose reduction or discontinuation [#ev:dopaminergic_withdrawal_caution:15500-16300]

05-000-4f94a46c.sentence: The choice between rechargeable and non‑rechargeable IPGs was significantly associated with affordability (χ²=19.13, p<0.001), and remote programming capability influenced brand selection [#ev:ev_609:400-1200].

05-001-ece7ab7c.sentence: Despite this, a preliminary survey of 17 DBS patients (85% of those approached) found that 76.5% used their patient programmer once per month or less, 17.7% never used it, and the most common function was checking battery life (58.8%) [#ev:ev_471:1400-2200].

05-002-4b723522.sentence: Adaptive DBS shows promise, as one study reported that overall well‑being significantly improved with adaptive stimulation (p=0.007) [#ev:ev_435:1000-1800].

05-003-9c9858e5.sentence: Caregivers of intermediate PD spent 6.6 hours per day caring, compared to 3.2 hours for early PD, and were less likely to be employed (25.3% vs 42.4%) [#ev:ev_040:5000-5800].

05-004-1c861e79.sentence: Personalized DBS programming using machine learning to optimize gait has produced meaningful improvements in walking, addressing a common post‑DBS challenge [#ev:ev_720:600-1400].

05-005-450f04ae.sentence: Remote patient monitoring devices led 79.9% of participants to agree that they improved mobility, and 48% perceived themselves as safer [#ev:ev_722:1500-2300].

05-006-961e7d90.sentence: However, a meta‑synthesis of wearable device experiences identified barriers including discomfort, emotional distress, and interface difficulties that hinder long‑term adherence [#ev:ev_719:100-900].

05-007-27b6f2db.sentence: 87.3% of DBS patients used rechargeable IPGs (r-IPGs), 87.7% were satisfied with their device; among those with r-IPGs, 40.6% felt confident handling the device within one week of discharge, 71.4% recharged independently, and the mean recharge interval was 4.3 days [#ev:ev_609:1800-2600]

05-008-90393952.sentence: Caregivers of people with advanced PD had significantly higher perceived burden (ZBI 34.5 vs 24.7 for early PD) and were more likely to take medication due to caregiving (34.7% vs 13.8%) [#ev:ev_040:6100-6900].

05-009-38578649.sentence: Assistive technologies also contribute: a scoping review reported that home automation significantly increased quality of life (p<0.001), and 63.5% of participants used speech‑to‑text functions to cope with tremors [#ev:ev_722:1200-2000].
